articles.title: TVS Starts Production of India-Made Norton Atlas Middleweight ADV Bike at Hosur Plant
articles.description: TVS Motor Company has started production of the Norton Atlas at its Hosur facility with the first unit completing assembly prior to its global launch next month. This move establishes India as the main manufacturing hub for Norton bikes intended for international markets.

articles.title: MEA passport remark row: BJP fires back at Opposition with 'kagaz nahin dikhayenge' swipe, cites court ruling
articles.description: It is worth noting that MEA's statement has not just triggered a political slugfest, social media too, witnessed a meltdown with users questioning the remark.

articles.title: Owner paid for repair despite warranty; court orders Mahindra, dealer 34K payout
articles.description: A Himachal Pradesh consumer commission has ordered Mahindra and its dealer to refund over Rs 19,000 to a vehicle owner. The pickup truck broke down, and the owner was forced to pay for repairs despite it being under warranty. The commission found the companies liable for deficiency in service, rejecting their claims of driver abuse and delayed service. The owner also gets compensation for mental agony and litigation costs.

articles.title: Box Office: Samantha's Maa Inti Bangaaram Nears Rs 60 Crore Worldwide
articles.description: Maa Inti Bangaaram's worldwide gross collection has now reached Rs 55.71 crore

articles.title: Tomato Prices Jump 24%: Is A Weak Monsoon The Start Of A Bigger Food Inflation Problem?
articles.description:
India's tomato prices are climbing once again, bringing back memories of the sharp spikes that have periodically strained household budgets over the past few years. While tomatoes have always been among the country's most volatile food items, this time the price rally is being driven by a combination of extreme weather, patchy monsoon rains and disruptions in supplies from key producing states.

According to data from the Department of Consumer Affairs' Price Monitoring Cell, the average retail price of tomatoes rose to Rs 43.7 per kg on Wednesday, up 24 per cent from a year earlier, reported Financial Express. Prices have also increased 26 per cent over the past month as supplies failed to keep pace with demand.

The surge comes at a time when the southwest monsoon has made a weak start. According to the India Meteorological Department (IMD), rainfall is currently running 43 per cent below normal, prompting concerns over crop conditions during the crucial kharif sowing season. Agriculture Minister Shivraj Singh Chouhan has also warned that 315 districts are expected to receive below-normal rainfall, raising questions over the outlook for several rain-fed crops, reported Outlook India.

Although the immediate impact is being felt in tomatoes, experts say the current trend offers an early indication of how weather disruptions can quickly feed into food inflation.

Tomato Prices Feel the Heat

Traders say supplies from major tomato-growing regions, including Maharashtra, Karnataka and Uttar Pradesh, have been affected by a combination of heatwaves and deficient rainfall.

Ashok Kaushik of Delhi's Azadpur Mandi, one of India's largest wholesale fruit and vegetable markets, told FE that supplies from Uttar Pradesh and Haryana have nearly stopped, while growers in Karnataka and Maharashtra are finding better prices in southern markets.

The disruption has pushed wholesale prices sharply higher. According to the Department of Consumer Affairs, the modal wholesale tomato price increased to around Rs 3,000 per quintal, nearly 50 per cent higher than a year ago.

At Azadpur, wholesale prices have climbed to about Rs 2,700 per quintal, representing a 66 per cent year-on-year increase, according to the traders.

The situation is even more pronounced at Andhra Pradesh's Madanapalle Agricultural Market, regarded as one of Asia's largest tomato trading hubs. Traders there say wholesale prices have crossed Rs 34,000 per quintal this week compared with around Rs 2,000 per quintal a month ago, as buyers from multiple states compete for limited arrivals.

Also Read: Falling Oil Prices And Global Tech Rally Drive Gains; Sensex Jumps 350 Points, Nifty Over 24K

Why Weather Is Playing Such a Big Role

Tomatoes are among the first vegetables to respond to weather disruptions because of their relatively short production cycle and highly regional supply chain.

According to an official note from the Agriculture Ministry, tomato production is spread across nearly 18 states, including Madhya Pradesh, Gujarat, Maharashtra, Chhattisgarh, Odisha, West Bengal, Telangana, Karnataka, Tamil Nadu and Punjab.

This geographical spread generally helps stabilise supplies throughout the year. However, the ministry notes that production disruptions in even a few key states can quickly affect the national supply chain.

The Department of Consumer Affairs has also identified June-August and October-November as lean production periods for tomatoes, when seasonal shortages often lead to price increases.

This year's weather has amplified that seasonal vulnerability.

The IMD has already forecast a below-normal monsoon for 2026, the first such forecast in 11 years, according to Reuters. The weather office has also indicated that weak rainfall is likely to continue in the near term, increasing uncertainty for farmers during the early stages of kharif cultivation.

Monsoon Deficit Raises Broader Concerns

While tomatoes are currently dominating headlines, policymakers are watching the broader agricultural picture closely.

Following a review of monsoon preparedness, Agriculture Minister Shivraj Singh Chouhan said the government has identified 315 districts across states including Maharashtra, Madhya Pradesh, Gujarat, Rajasthan, Karnataka, Uttar Pradesh, Bihar, Telangana, Andhra Pradesh and Odisha that are likely to receive below-normal rainfall.

Among them, 111 districts have been categorised as the most vulnerable because less than one-fourth of their agricultural land is irrigated, making crops heavily dependent on rainfall.

The timing is significant because June and July mark the peak sowing period for kharif crops such as rice, maize, soybean, cotton and sugarcane. While overall kharif sowing has marginally exceeded last year's pace, soybean remains the notable exception, according to Agriculture Ministry data.

Despite these concerns, the government has maintained that there is no reason for panic at this stage, stressing that contingency plans have already been activated across vulnerable districts.

Government Bets on Contingency Measures

The Centre has moved quickly to prepare for a weaker monsoon, focusing on minimising the impact on both farm output and food supplies.

Speaking after reviewing monsoon preparedness, Chouhan said the government has drawn up state-specific contingency plans for districts expected to receive below-normal rainfall. The plans encourage farmers in rain-fed regions to shift towards short-duration and less water-intensive crops, including pulses, oilseeds and coarse cereals.

"There is a gap in rainfall. We need to suggest to farmers to go for alternative crops. We will not let farm fields remain empty," Chouhan said.

The minister also stressed that seed and fertiliser availability remains adequate and that the government is working to ensure agricultural production does not decline despite the weather challenges.

To strengthen preparedness, the Agriculture Ministry has asked its network of 731 Krishi Vigyan Kendras (KVKs) to step up farmer outreach through SMS alerts, WhatsApp groups, call centres and local advisories. States have also been directed to improve water conservation by cleaning ponds, farm reservoirs, streams and check dams under the Viksit Bharat–Guarantee for Rozgar and Ajeevika Mission Gramin (VB-GRAM G) programme.

In addition, an El Niño Monitoring Cell and a Crop Weather Watch Group have been established to monitor rainfall patterns and provide real-time advisories.

Also Read: Digital Gold Is Trending, Why Young Indians Are Changing The Way They Invest In Gold

Food Inflation Could Face Fresh Pressure

The sharp increase in tomato prices comes at a time when food inflation remains under close watch.

According to the Ministry of Statistics, tomato inflation stood at 48.43 per cent in May, contributing to food inflation of 4.78 per cent during the month. Economists note that vegetables often respond quickly to weather-related supply disruptions, making them one of the earliest indicators of changing food price trends.

Whether the current increase develops into a broader inflationary cycle will depend largely on how the monsoon progresses over the coming weeks.

For now, the government has pointed to comfortable stocks of staple grains such as rice and wheat, which could help cushion any immediate supply shocks. It has also expanded access to crop insurance, farm credit and income-support schemes in districts considered vulnerable to deficient rainfall.

Not Every Kitchen Staple Is Turning Costlier

Tomatoes may be grabbing attention, but the price trend across vegetables remains mixed.

Department of Consumer Affairs data show that the average retail price of potatoes has declined 14 per cent from a year earlier to Rs 21.99 per kg. Onion prices have increased only marginally to Rs 28.05 per kg.

Traders attribute this relative stability to healthy rabi production and adequate stocks, which are expected to meet demand until fresh early kharif arrivals begin reaching markets later in the year.

This contrast underlines an important feature of India's food economy: while weather shocks can send prices of perishable vegetables soaring within weeks, crops with stronger inventories and longer storage life tend to remain more stable.

Also Read : Oil Prices Tumble As Strait Of Hormuz Reopens: Is The Energy Crisis Over?

What Consumers Can Expect Next

Traders believe tomato prices are likely to remain elevated over the next few weeks until supplies from Maharashtra, Karnataka and Uttar Pradesh improve following better rainfall.

The pace of the monsoon will therefore remain the key factor determining whether the current price spike remains confined to tomatoes or begins affecting a wider range of food items.

articles.title: Tripura CM Manik Saha pushes for sustainable water strategy for future
articles.description: Tripura CM Manik Saha calls for a long-term, sustainable water management strategy for the entire state to meet future drinking water needs. A high-level meeting reviewed a proposed Gomati River-based water supply project for Agartala.

articles.title: Airline surge charges may be reviewed if ATF prices remain stable: Govt
articles.description: Union Civil Aviation Minister K Ram Mohan Naidu said the government may ask airlines to review surge charges if fuel prices show long-term stability. The Centre is monitoring ATF prices and is in talks with airlines to assess the recent decline.

articles.title: Emergency 'dark chapter' in India's history, say Union Ministers
articles.description: Union Ministers and senior leaders launched a strong attack on the 1975 Emergency, calling it a 'dark chapter' in India's history. They highlighted the suppression of constitutional rights, civil liberties, and freedom of expression.

articles.title: Kolkata godown collapse: PM Modi announces Rs 2 lakh ex-gratia
articles.description: PM Modi announced Rs 2 lakh ex-gratia for families of 8 deceased and Rs 50,000 for the 20 injured in Kolkata's godown shed collapse. The death toll stands at eight. Three people, including a supervisor and two labour suppliers, have been arrested.

articles.title: NCERT introduces Emergency in Class 9 textbook for first time, calls it 'challenge to democracy'
articles.description: NCERT Curriculum: Class 9 students now study the 1975 Emergency period, highlighting it as a major challenge to Indian democracy. New textbook sections discuss Fundamental Rights, censorship, and political arrests, fostering democratic understanding.

articles.title: Salmonella in apples and kiwi? Major UK supermarkets recall fruit products over potential contamination
articles.description: Major UK supermarkets including Tesco, Asda, and Sainsbury's are recalling apple and kiwi fruit packs due to potential Salmonella contamination. Shoppers are urged to check their fridges and return any affected products for a full refund. The recall was initiated by supplier Prepworld after detecting the bacteria. Food safety officials warn against consuming the fruit and advise seeking medical attention if symptoms arise.

articles.title: Clarke believes Scotland heading home after self-inflicted defeat
articles.description: MIAMI, June 24 : Scotland coach Steve Clarke thinks his team are heading home from their first World Cup in 28 years after what he viewed as a largely self-inflicted 3-0 loss to Brazil in their final Group C match on Wednesday. The Scots, with three points, still technically have a chance of progressing to t

articles.title: CM sets up women’s panel to tackle workplace conversion, harassment
articles.description: Fadnavis announced that a committee of women lawmakers will be constituted shortly to review the existing legal framework and recommend reforms, if needed

articles.title: Kolkata Warehouse Collapse: 5 Dead, Some Still Feared Trapped, SIT Formed As Rescue Ops Continue
articles.description: Rescuers race against time in Kolkata's Taratala warehouse collapse as five deaths are confirmed, an SIT is formed, and several workers are still feared trapped.

articles.title: Allentown Fire: Massive Blaze Triggers Fifth Alarm After Partial Building Collapse
articles.description: A massive fire ripped through a block-long three-storey structure in Allentown, Pennsylvania, prompting authorities to escalate the response to a fifth alarm.

articles.title: Sena (UBT) MPs meet Lok Sabha speaker Birla, seek copy of demands by six rebel leaders
articles.description: After the meeting with Lok Sabha speaker, Sena (UBT) MPs said they had requested the Speaker to “uphold the provisions of the Constitution”.

articles.title: Venezuela In Rubble: Earthquake Damages Maiquetia Airport, Flattens Buildings In La Guaira | WATCH VIDEOS
articles.description: A significant earthquake has caused severe damage at Simón Bolívar International Airport in Venezuela, leading to the collapse of part of the terminal's roof. Videos depict extensive destruction, including fallen ceiling panels and debris scattered

articles.title: Narwekar apologises after criticism over tribute speech
articles.description: MNS chief Raj Thackeray had criticised Narwekar for what he termed the “butchering” of the Marathi language and for being unable to correctly read a prepared text.

articles.title: Top stocks in news: Airtel, LIC, Tata Steel, HCL Tech, ICICI Bank, IRFC, Vedanta, NLC India
articles.description: Stocks like Bharti Airtel, LIC of India, Tata Steel, HCL Tech, ICICI Bank, IRFC, Vedanta, NLC India, Bharat Forge, Sterlite Tech and more will be in the spotlight on Thursday, June 25.

articles.title: Venezuela Earthquake Live Updates: Acting president Delcy Rodríguez declares state of emergency after 2 back-to-back quakes
articles.description: A third earthquake of magnitude 7 hit Japan, jolting Hashikami Town, Aomori Prefecture. Its epicenter was off the coast of Iwate Prefecture.

articles.title: A dividend India must not squander
articles.description: The road to Viksit Bharat runs through India’s labour market. The country’s demographic dividend (DD) offers a rare opportunity to accelerate growth and prosperity. As the working-age share of the population rises, and the share of dependents (those below 15, and over 60 or 64 years) falls, several benefits emerge for the economy.

articles.title: Strawberry Moon, Mercury Retrograde, Jupiter in Leo and Mars in Gemini bring major shifts, according to an astrologer
articles.description: According to a US-based astrologer, the week between June 28 and July 4, 2026, could be one of the busiest periods in Western astrology.

articles.title: QCOM Stock Jumps 15% After-Hours — Massive AI Revenue Targets And Meta Deal Power Qualcomm Rally
articles.description: Qualcomm’s investor day highlighted a major shift toward AI, with a new Meta partnership and strong long-term revenue targets across its core businesses.

articles.title: MCG mandates metal barricades at Gurugram work sites to curb dust pollution
articles.description: Projects have 15 days to comply. Violators risk stop-work notices, sealing, utility disconnections, environmental compensation and blacklisting.

articles.title: ‘Lunatic’: Trump, Republican Senator Engage In Shouting Match Over Iran War
articles.description: Donald Trump and Sen. Bill Cassidy clashed in a heated closed door GOP lunch over Iran war powers, with a shouting match highlighting Republican tensions over Trump’s authority

articles.title: FIFA World Cup: Switzerland wraps up first place in Group B with 2-1 victory over Canada
articles.description: Switzerland secures Group B's top spot with a 2-1 win over Canada, both advancing to the World Cup knockout stage.

articles.title: Register pet dogs or face action: MCG tells owners in Gurugram
articles.description: Owners must leash pets in public, use muzzles where required and carry poop bags. Registration costs ₹500, while existing licences remain valid.

articles.title: Strong Earthquake Strikes West Of Caracas, Venezuela
articles.description: A powerful 7.1-magnitude earthquake struck western Venezuela near Morón, with tremors felt across Caracas, the Caribbean, including Aruba and Curaçao.

articles.title: FIFA World Cup: Bosnia-Herzegovina boosts chances of advancing with 3-1 win over Qatar
articles.description: Bosnia-Herzegovina's 3-1 victory over Qatar enhances their World Cup advancement chances, marking a historic moment for the team.

articles.title: Global Market: China's 'future industries' investment boom sparks valuation bubble concerns
articles.description: China is witnessing a massive surge in venture capital, pouring billions into futuristic sectors like space, quantum computing, and AI. This investment frenzy, driven by Beijing's ambition to lead in technology and reduce reliance on foreign tech, sees even revenue-less startups attracting significant funding. Investor confidence is high, with new listing rules easing domestic market access for these promising, government-backed ventures.

articles.title: Best proverb of the day: 'When a rich man becomes poor he becomes a...' - this Chinese saying reveals how setbacks become powerful life lessons for others and why loss and failure can become your greatest teacher?
articles.description: Best proverb of the day: Explore the profound wisdom of a Chinese proverb that teaches how setbacks in wealth can lead to incredible life lessons, resilience, and teaching opportunities for those who have experienced both riches and poorness.

articles.title: Inspirational proverb of the day: “He who deliberates fully before taking a step will... — Life lessons on action, courage, overthinking and why a journey of a thousand miles begins with a single step
articles.description: Inspirational proverb of the day highlights the meaning of “He who deliberates fully before taking a step will spend his entire life on one leg.” The Chinese saying reflects how overthinking, hesitation, and excessive caution can sometimes prevent people from moving forward in life. It suggests that constant analysis of every possible outcome may lead to delay, causing missed opportunities for growth and progress.

articles.title: SFI names four-member Indian surfing team for 2026 Asian Games
articles.description: The Surfing Federation of India named a four-member team for surfing's debut at the 2026 Asian Games. Kishore Kumar, Sivaraj Babu, Kamali Moorthy, and Sugar Shanthi Banarse were selected based on performance in national and international events.

articles.title: Tata-STT's Delhi data centre suffers 'extensive damage'; data recovery challenged
articles.description: A significant fire at a New Delhi data centre, a joint venture between ST Telemedia and Tata Communications, has caused extensive damage, complicating data recovery efforts. One affected client, Matrix Cellular, reports losing two decades of crucial data. The incident also appears linked to recent intermittent Google Cloud network issues in India, leaving server rooms in ruins.

articles.title: Uttar Pradesh roadshow in Bengaluru nets Rs 50,000 crore in investment pledges
articles.description: Uttar Pradesh Chief Minister Yogi Adityanath held a series of investor meetings in Bengaluru on Wednesday as part of a roadshow to attract investment into the state, which he says is now among India's top three investment destinations.

articles.title: IPL 2026: Rishabh Pant-Kuldeep Yadav trade 'good for both teams'
articles.description: India pacer Avesh Khan backs the Rishabh Pant-Kuldeep Yadav trade between Lucknow Super Giants and Delhi Capitals, calling it a beneficial move for both franchises. He believes Kuldeep's spin will be a significant asset for LSG at their home ground.

articles.title: Deepender links arrest of IAS officer to RS poll
articles.description: The IDFC First Bank scam was of such magnitude that the CBI could not overlook it,” Mr. Hooda claimed.

articles.title: 40-year-old share trader found dead in city hotel, family alleges murder
articles.description: Jaipur trader Guru Prasad Chaudhary found dead in Narayan Vihar hotel room; police probe friends, Facebook Live video, and alcohol angle after family files FIR.

articles.title: SPG Director Alok Sharma gets nine-month service extension till 2027
articles.description: The Central government has extended the service of Special Protection Group (SPG) Director Alok Sharma by nine months. The 1991-batch IPS officer will now serve until March 31, 2027. The ACC approved the extension beyond his scheduled retirement.

articles.title: Govt pattas undercut crackdown on illegal colonies
articles.description: Rajasthan govt issues pattas via Urban Service Camps to regularise unauthorised colonies, easing inspections—officials warn it encourages encroachments and unsafe layouts.

articles.title: Mohan Yadav at centre of Ujjain land ‘scam’; BJP bombshell on Kharge family's trust: What is happening?
articles.description: A report showed that Mohan Yadav’s family bought 168 acres for ₹45 crore since December 2023, when the BJP leader become Madhya Pradesh chief minister.

articles.title: 'Everybody’s sick of you': New book claims Trump exploded at Netanyahu during heated Gaza ceasefire call
articles.description: A new book claims Trump exploded at Netanyahu during Gaza ceasefire talks in 2025. The reported outburst exposed deep private tensions despite their public alliance.

articles.title: Mysuru RPF officers trained on Jan Vishwas Act, 2026
articles.description: A briefing-cum-awareness session on the provisions of the Jan Vishwas (Amendment of Provisions) Act, 2026 and the consequential amendments to the Railways Act, 1989 and the Calcutta Metro Railway (Operation and Maintenance) Temporary Provisions Act, 1985 was conducted on Wednesday in Mysuru for officers and staff of the Railway Protection Force (RPF) of Mysuru and Hubballi divisions.

articles.title: China to enforce new ethnic unity law globally, says it has right to target 'separatists'
articles.description: The Ethnic Unity and Progress Promotion Law, which comes into force on July 1, seeks to strengthen what Beijing describes as a shared national identity among the country's ethnic groups. One provision extends potential legal liability to people and organisations outside China, an aspect that has drawn particular scrutiny. Rights advocates warn that the measure could provide fresh legal grounds for targeting overseas campaigners from communities such as the Uyghurs and Tibetans.

articles.title: `When Villages Progress, Punjab Progresses`: CM Bhagwant Singh Mann
articles.description: The AAP Chief said the decision reflects the Bhagwant Mann Government’s commitment to empowering grassroots democracy, strengthening rural governance and accelerating village development, as the government continues to take people-centric decisions aimed at building a prosperous and Rangla Punjab.

articles.title: Tripura suspends 8 policemen after HC observation in custodial torture & bribery case
articles.description: Tripura suspends 8 policemen after HC observation in custodial torture & bribery case

articles.title: 19 BJP councillors retake oath in Thiruvananthapuram Corporation after Kerala HC invalidates earlier ceremony
articles.description: 19 BJP councillors in Thiruvananthapuram Corporation have retaken their oaths following a High Court ruling that invalidated their previous ones. The court mandated strict adherence to the prescribed format, disallowing any additions like deities or "Mother India." This decision stems from challenges to oaths taken by 20 BJP councillors who had invoked Hindu deities and political martyrs. The BJP currently holds a majority in the 101-member civic body.

articles.title: School Education Minister reviews progress of various govt. schemes in Perambalur district
articles.description: School Education Minister Rajmohan reviews government schemes in Perambalur, emphasizing impact assessment and readiness for challenges like drought.

articles.title: ‘EVERYBODY HATES YOU!’: Trump Explodes At Netanyahu With ‘DIVORCE’ Threat In Secret Call | Report
articles.description: Did Donald Trump threaten to 'divorce' Israeli prime minister Benjamin Netanyahu during an angry phone call? A new book is shedding light on an alleged private clash between the two leaders, claiming what could be one of the most serious rifts in their long-standing political relationship. According to Regime Change: Inside the Imperial Presidency of Donald Trump by New York Times journalists Maggie Haberman and Jonathan Swan, Trump erupted during a September 2025 phone call over Netanyahu’s reluctance to accept a U.S.-brokered Gaza ceasefire plan. The book claims Trump told Netanyahu that “everybody hates you” and warned that continued opposition to the deal could lead to a “divorce” between the United States and Israel. The conversation reportedly took place in the presence of Trump envoy Steve Witkoff and son-in-law Jared Kushner.

articles.title: UP emerged as revenue surplus state overcoming 'Bimaru' status, says CM Adityanath
articles.description: "For the last six years, Uttar Pradesh has been a revenue-surplus state. Rising from the bottom three, Uttar Pradesh is now counted among India's top three economies," CM said.
